routes.name: Bank SWIFT Inward Remittance

costRange.summary: Indicative bank charge and FX spread range; not a live quote.

estimatedTimeline.summary: Commonly settles after bank screening, intermediary processing, and documentation review.

documentation.summary: Strong documentation trail for export receipts, with more manual bank review.
requirements: FIRC or e-BRC record where applicable

baseRiskFlags.summary: Intermediary banks can add deductions or request extra payment details.
Bank review may pause settlement when invoice, purpose, or counterparty details are incomplete.

routes.tradeoffSummary: Familiar bank-led route with a strong audit trail, but usually slower and more operationally heavy than specialist collection routes.

routes.name: Fintech-Assisted Export Collection

costRange.summary: Indicative platform and FX spread range; not a live quote.

estimatedTimeline.summary: Often faster once customer, invoice, and remittance details are complete.

documentation.summary: Useful for recurring export collections when invoice and customer context are consistent.

baseRiskFlags.summary: Platform limits or enhanced due diligence may apply for new or high-value customers.
Finance teams should map platform records back to invoice and export reporting records.

routes.tradeoffSummary: Usually faster and easier to operate than bank-only SWIFT, with care needed to keep export documentation and settlement records aligned.

routes.name: Local Receiving Account For Export Proceeds

costRange.summary: Indicative collection and conversion range; not a live quote.

estimatedTimeline.summary: Can be quick for repeat customers after account and payer details are set up.

documentation.summary: Best for repeat export receipts where payer and invoice patterns are stable.

baseRiskFlags.summary: Payer names should match commercial records to avoid reconciliation and compliance questions.
Collection records must remain traceable to invoices and export reporting evidence.

routes.tradeoffSummary: Often the simplest recurring export collection path, but it relies on clean reconciliation between payer, invoice, and settlement records.

routes.name: Bank SWIFT Outward Remittance

costRange.summary: Indicative bank fee and FX spread range; not a live quote.

estimatedTimeline.summary: Typical timing depends on bank cutoffs, correspondent banks, and beneficiary bank review.

documentation.summary: Conservative route for vendor and trade payments when bank review and audit trail matter most.
requirements: Import or service documentation where applicable

baseRiskFlags.summary: Incorrect beneficiary or correspondent details can delay or return payments.
Submission after bank cutoff can push settlement into the next business day.

routes.tradeoffSummary: Widely accepted and documentation-heavy, with slower timing and less predictable deductions than local or specialist routes.

routes.name: Fintech-Assisted Outward Transfer

costRange.summary: Indicative platform and FX spread range; not a live quote.

estimatedTimeline.summary: Often faster after vendor, invoice, and compliance details are pre-validated.

documentation.summary: Useful for repeat vendor payments where the counterparty and purpose are predictable.

baseRiskFlags.summary: Route limits or additional review may apply based on amount, vendor, or payment purpose.
Finance should retain platform confirmations with invoice and accounting records.

routes.tradeoffSummary: A practical middle path for cost and speed, with documentation discipline needed for repeatable compliance records.

routes.name: Fintech Local-Rails Payout

costRange.summary: Indicative local payout and FX spread range; not a live quote.

estimatedTimeline.summary: Can be quick when both corridor and beneficiary details are supported.

documentation.summary: Best for recurring vendor payouts where local beneficiary details are stable.

baseRiskFlags.summary: Local payout availability depends on beneficiary country, currency, and account details.
Beneficiary names should match vendor records to reduce hold or return risk.

routes.tradeoffSummary: Potentially lower-cost and faster for repeat UAE or Singapore vendor payouts, but eligibility is narrower than bank SWIFT.

routes.name: Bank Trade Remittance With Document Review

costRange.summary: Indicative trade remittance and FX spread range; not a live quote.

estimatedTimeline.summary: Slower because payment review is tied to trade documents and bank processing windows.

documentation.summary: Most appropriate when the payment depends on formal import, trade, or shipment evidence.
requirements: Bill of entry, airway bill, or shipment evidence where applicable

baseRiskFlags.summary: Mismatches between invoice, shipment, and beneficiary details can lead to bank queries.
Trade documentation review can extend the payment timeline.

routes.tradeoffSummary: Highest documentation burden, but useful when the finance team needs a bank-reviewed trade record for complex import or shipment-linked payments.
